message:
feat: add rectangular SVD/QR, negative-stride support, and N-dimensional FFT

fnp-linalg: Rectangular matrix decompositions
- Add qr_mxn() for QR decomposition of arbitrary m×n matrices using
  Householder reflections, returning full (complete mode) Q (m×m) and
  upper-trapezoidal R (m×n). The existing qr_nxn() only handled square
  matrices, but NumPy's np.linalg.qr accepts rectangular inputs.
- Add svd_mxn() for computing singular values of m×n matrices via QR
  iteration on A^T*A. Returns min(m,n) singular values in descending
  order, matching NumPy's np.linalg.svd(compute_uv=False) behavior.
- Add svd_mxn_full() for full SVD decomposition (U, S, Vt) of m×n
  matrices. Recovers U columns as A*V/sigma with Gram-Schmidt
  orthogonalization for the null-space columns when m > min(m,n).
  This matches NumPy's np.linalg.svd(full_matrices=True).
- Add comprehensive tests: tall (3x2) and wide (2x3) QR reconstruction
  and orthogonality checks, SVD singular value validation against known
  values, full SVD reconstruction (U*diag(S)*Vt ≈ A), identity matrix
  SVD, and input validation (empty, NaN rejection).

fnp-ndarray: Negative stride support for as_strided views
- Rewrite required_view_nbytes() to correctly compute the byte span of
  views that use negative strides. Previously, any negative stride was
  rejected with ShapeError::NegativeStride, which prevented reverse
  slicing (arr[::-1]) and flipped axis views. The new implementation
  tracks both max_offset (from positive stride contributions) and
  min_offset (from negative stride contributions) as signed isize
  values, then computes span = max_offset - min_offset + item_size.
  This enables NumPy-compatible negative-stride views while still
  rejecting out-of-bounds spans via the existing OutOfBoundsView check.
- Update tests: the old test asserting NegativeStride rejection is
  replaced with tests that verify negative strides work for 1-D reverse
  (vec![4], strides=[-8]), 2-D row-flip (vec![3,3], strides=[-24,8]),
  and that truly out-of-bounds negative-stride views are still rejected.

fnp-ufunc: N-dimensional FFT family (fftn, ifftn, rfftn, irfftn)
- Add fftn_along_axis() helper that applies a 1-D FFT (or inverse FFT)
  along a single axis of an N-dimensional flat row-major array. It
  extracts 1-D slices by computing the correct outer/inner strides,
  calls fft_dit() on each slice, and writes results back.
- Add fftn() (np.fft.fftn): applies 1-D FFT along every axis in order,
  returning interleaved complex output with trailing dimension 2.
- Add ifftn() (np.fft.ifftn): inverse of fftn, applies 1-D IFFT along
  every spatial axis.
- Add rfftn() (np.fft.rfftn): real-input N-D FFT that applies full FFT
  along all axes then truncates the last axis to n//2+1 frequencies,
  matching NumPy's Hermitian-symmetric optimization.
- Add irfftn() (np.fft.irfftn): inverse of rfftn that reconstructs the
  full spectrum via Hermitian symmetry before applying N-D IFFT, then
  returns real output only. Accepts optional last_n parameter for the
  output length along the last axis.
- Add tests: fftn matches fft on 1-D, fftn matches fft2 on 2-D,
  fftn/ifftn roundtrip on 2-D and 3-D data, rfftn matches rfft on 1-D,
  rfftn/irfftn roundtrip on 2-D data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
